Metadata.title: Kewei Zhan — Full-stack / AI Application Engineer
Metadata.description: Portfolio of Kewei Zhan, a full-stack and AI application engineer building distributed AI systems, backend platforms, and production-style developer tools.

Hero.tagline: I build production-oriented AI systems, backend platforms, and developer tools.
Hero.tagline2: Focused on LLM agents, backend infrastructure, and AI evaluation pipelines.

WhatIDo.heading: WHAT I DO /
WhatIDo.label: (Services)
WhatIDo.intro: I design and build production-oriented backend systems, AI agent workflows, and full-stack software products — engineering for reliability, not just functionality.

services.title: Distributed AI Systems
services.description: I design backend systems that connect APIs, async workers, databases, queues, monitoring, and deployment into production-style AI applications.

services.title: LLM / RAG Applications
services.description: I build LLM agent workflows, retrieval-augmented generation features, tool execution flows, and evaluation-friendly AI pipelines.

services.title: Full-stack Product Engineering
services.description: I develop end-to-end products with authentication, authorization, database persistence, frontend interfaces, and cloud deployment.

Works.heading: SELECTED WORKS /
Works.label: (PROJECTS)
Works.intro: Production-oriented AI and backend systems, built for reliability, observability, and real-world scale.

About.lead: I'm a Computer Engineering master's student at USC building production-oriented AI and full-stack systems.
About.p1: My work combines backend engineering, distributed task execution, AI pipeline evaluation, and frontend product interfaces. Recently focused on LLM agents, bilingual ASR evaluation, and production-style developer platforms.
About.p2: B.S. Computer Science, Virginia Tech (minor in HCI). M.S. Computer Engineering, USC — expected Spring 2026. Co-authored a publication on AI-based license plate recognition, indexed by EI / Scopus / CPCI.

Contact.heading: Let's Work Together
Contact.subheading: Open to full-time software engineering roles in 2026.
